forecaster_note: Solid surf but S/SE winds — sheltered southern corners for the fun-sized clean stuff. Well overhead to 2x overhead at the south exposures, but onshore and messy on the open beaches all day. INCOMING: a solid winter swell — Tue & Wed the standout clean overhead-plus mornings, Thu a possible renewal in size, Fri cleaning up as winds swing W. — Tim Williams (Surfline)

wind.summary: S 21kph (34 gusts) — onshore-to-cross and lumpy on the open beaches; tuck into the sheltered southern corners, and go early before it builds

shark.summary: Two drone shark sightings on the Northern Beaches yesterday morning (Sun 5 Jul): Newport 8:08am and Warriewood 8:09am, both water-evacuated. Neither at your tracked breaks (Dee Why, Long Reef, Curl Curl, Manly) and nothing overnight — but sharks are about with this S-swell push, so stay aware and check the flags.

days.label: Today
days.note: Messy S-wind day with plenty of size in the water (11ft @ 11s SSE, 166°). The open beaches are onshore-to-cross and lumpy — the play is a sheltered southern corner early, before the S'ly fully fills in. Dee Why Point tucks in for clean frontside rights; Curl Curl's south end is the other option. Your 10–12 window will be catching the building bump, so the earlier the better. Two shark drone sightings up the coast yesterday AM — none at your breaks, but scan the lineup.

dee-why.window: Sheltered play — the open bank is cross-shore and lumpy. Tuck into Dee Why Point early for your frontside rights before the S wind fills in. Fun overhead corners on the sets.

tip.label: Point over beachbreak
tip.text: 741kJ and 100/100 consistency means the sets keep marching, but it's all SSE 166° energy the open bank chops up in this S wind. The Point is the answer — it shelters from the S'ly and hands you clean frontside rights. Be there before 9 while it's cleanest.

long-reef.window: Biggest, rawest option — 1088kJ of S swell straight onto the outer reefs, but the S wind is onshore and it's messy. Experienced crew, dawn only.

tip.label: Too much, too messy
tip.text: Long Reef soaks up every bit of this S swell — 1088kJ, 6–8ft and building — but the wind sits straight onshore here. Big and disorganised, not the clean 4–5ft-with-shape you want. Bookmark it for Tue when winds ease.

curl-curl.window: S-swell magnet loaded up (907kJ) but onshore-affected. The south end / Curl Curl corner is the cleaner nook early before the wind builds.

tip.label: South corner or skip
tip.text: Curly eats S swell so the size is there — 907kJ — but the open beach is onshore and lumpy. Tuck into the south end for rights off the rocks (your frontside) early, otherwise it's a wash.

manly.window: Smallest and most sheltered — cross-shore wind, head-high on the sets. Cleaner than the open beaches but soft. Backup if you want mellow.

tip.label: Sheltered but small
tip.text: North-facing Manly sees the least of this SSE swell — 540kJ, 2–4ft — but it's the most wind-protected corner. Fine for a clean cruisy paddle; for real size and punch the S magnets have it, just messier.

days.label: Tomorrow
days.note: The standout clean day — Tim's calling a quality morning, overhead to 2x-overhead across most breaks as the S swell holds and winds back off. Dee Why 4–6ft, the reefs a solid 6–8ft. Your 10–12 window should score if you want size with shape; dawn for the glassiest.

dee-why.window: Quality morning — 4–6ft with cleaner winds. The Point and the open banks both on offer; this is your day for size with shape. Early is glassiest.

long-reef.window: Serious size for the outer reefs — 6–8ft+. Experienced crew, dawn before any S'ly returns. Step-up territory.

curl-curl.window: Loaded S-magnet at 6–8ft with a cleaner morning — the south corner for the more makeable walls.

manly.window: Finally sees real size — 4–5ft head-high-plus. Fun rippable option if the reefs feel too heavy.

days.label: Weekend
days.note: The week's big S swell fades by the weekend. Saturday: SE energy easing to 2–3ft but an offshore morning window means clean, fun-size surf across the region — your kind of Saturday session. Sunday drops smaller (1–2ft), groveller territory. Sat AM is the pick.

dee-why.window: Sat: clean 2–3ft in the offshore morning window — fun rippable session on the daily driver. Sun smaller. Punchy-beachie territory.

long-reef.window: Sat: 2–4ft with residual S energy, cleanest early. Sun eases to 1–3ft.

curl-curl.window: Sat: 2–3ft clean in the morning window — the S-magnet holds a touch more. Sun small.

manly.window: Sat: mellow 2–3ft, most sheltered. Sun 1–2ft — grovel only.
